mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate cut periods, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 55% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving sustained equity appreciation over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during periods of high inflation signal supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression, causing immediate negative equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation periods yield positive 5-day forward returns in > 55% of instances, or the regime-specific predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates real rate compression, which stimulates risk appetite and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The real rate differential fails to predict 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or positive real rate gaps consistently precede negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls reflect weakening economic momentum and reduced corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.03, or PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 60-day returns in > 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent signals robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of instances following GDP prints above 3.0%, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during periods of monetary tightening.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during late-cycle expansions signal capacity utilization exhaustion and impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines in late-cycle regimes yield positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the mechanism's predictive power disappears when the yield curve is steeply positive.
